137	### Citation discipline (the hard rule)
138	
139	**Every declaration block that derives from external reference material requires three citation elements:**

8	/-!
9	# Flattening stratification of a coherent sheaf (A.2.a)
10	
11	This file is the **A.2.a** file-skeleton sub-build chapter for the project's
12	positive-genus arm of `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It packages the
13	Nitsure~\S4 flattening-stratification theorem — the technical heart that
14	turns the absolute Quot-functor representability problem on a projective
15	morphism `π : X ⟶ S` with coherent input `𝓕` into a finite collection of
16	locally-closed strata over which `𝓕` becomes flat.
17	
18	## Status (iter-176 Lane E file-skeleton — re-dispatch)
19	
20	iter-175 Lane E died to the Anthropic session-limit reset window without
21	ever calling `Write` (the file was never created). iter-176 re-dispatches
22	the file-skeleton verbatim. Each blueprint-pinned declaration carries the
23	*intended* substantive type signature (matching the `\lean{...}` pin in
24	`blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_FlatteningStratification.tex`) with a
25	`sorry` body. The bodies are iter-177+ work; the substantive proofs are
26	deep (Nitsure~\S4 ~250-line argument: generic-flatness ⟶ Noetherian
27	induction ⟶ Castelnuovo–Mumford regularity ⟶ assembly via direct images
28	`E_i = π_* 𝓕(N+i)`).
29	
30	The 4 blueprint-pinned declarations are:
31	
32	1. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat` (def, ~8 LOC) — the
33	   **coherent-sheaf flatness predicate** for a coherent `𝓕` on `X` over
34	   `S` via `f : X ⟶ S`. Affine-locally: for `U ⊆ S` and `V ⊆ f⁻¹U` both
35	   affine, `Γ(𝓕, V)` is a flat `Γ(S, U)`-module via the composite
36	   `Γ(S, U) → Γ(X, V) → End(Γ(𝓕, V))`. The corresponding Stacks tag is
37	   00HB (module-level) lifted to a sheaf-level predicate by the
38	   affine-local descent characterisation.
39	
40	2. `AlgebraicGeometry.genericFlatness` (theorem, ~15 LOC) — **generic
41	   flatness** [Nitsure §4 Theorem]: over a noetherian integral base
42	   `S`, a coherent sheaf on a finite-type `X ⟶ S` is flat over a
43	   non-empty open `V ⊆ S`.
44	
45	3. `AlgebraicGeometry.flatteningStratification` (theorem, ~25 LOC) — the
46	   **flattening-stratification existence theorem** [Nitsure §4]: for `S`
47	   noetherian and `𝓕` coherent on a proper `π : X ⟶ S`, there exists a
48	   finite locally-closed stratification `{S_f}` of `S` (set-theoretically
49	   covering, pairwise disjoint) such that `𝓕` becomes flat on each
50	   stratum.
51	
52	4. `AlgebraicGeometry.flatteningStratification_universal` (theorem, ~15 LOC)
53	   — the **universal property** of the stratification [Nitsure §4 (ii)]:
54	   any morphism `φ : T ⟶ S` such that `𝓕` pulled back to `X ×_S T` is
55	   `T`-flat factors uniquely through the stratum coproduct
56	   `i : ⨿ S_f ⟶ S`.
57	
58	Additionally, three sub-lemmas decompose the existence proof, plus a
59	curve-specialisation corollary feeding the Route~A consumer:
60	
61	5. `AlgebraicGeometry.flatLocusStratification` (lemma, ~12 LOC) — the
62	   **special case `n = 0`** [Nitsure §4, n=0]: a coherent `𝓕` on `S`
63	   admits a finite locally-closed stratification by integer "rank"
64	   strata.
65	
66	6. `AlgebraicGeometry.flatLocusReduction` (lemma, ~12 LOC) — the
67	   **Noetherian-induction reduction** [Nitsure §4 general case opening]:
68	   a finite reduced locally-closed stratification on which `𝓕`
69	   *already* becomes flat (without polynomial indexing).
70	
71	7. `AlgebraicGeometry.flatLocusAssembly` (lemma, ~15 LOC) — the
72	   **assembly step** [Nitsure §4 via direct images]: combining the
73	   `n = 0` stratification iteratively on the direct images
74	   `E_i = π_* 𝓕(N+i)` to refine to the Hilbert-polynomial-indexed
75	   stratification.
76	
77	8. `AlgebraicGeometry.flatteningStratification.ofCurve` (theorem, ~10 LOC)
78	   — the **relative-curve specialisation** for the Route~A consumer
79	   `C ×_k T → T`.
80	
81	## Note on type expressivity
82	
83	Following the project rule "Never weaken the type to dodge the proof",
84	each declaration carries a substantive, non-tautological type:
85	
86	- `CoherentSheafFlat` reduces to module-theoretic flatness of every
87	  affine-local section module over the corresponding affine-base ring
88	  via the `appLE` ring-hom — non-empty content because flatness of a
89	  module is the standard non-trivial homological condition.
90	- `genericFlatness` requires the existence of a *non-empty* open `V`
91	  and substantive flatness on every affine `U ⊆ V`; both quantifiers
92	  are necessary (without them the statement collapses to `V = ∅`).
93	- `flatteningStratification` packages a *finite* indexed family of
94	  *locally-closed-immersions* `ι : S_f ⟶ S` that pairwise *cover*
95	  `|S|` *disjointly* AND on which the pullback `𝓕|_{X ×_S S_f}` becomes
96	  `S_f`-flat — all four conjuncts are substantive (any single
97	  one trivialises if dropped).
98	- `flatteningStratification_universal` packages the universal property
99	  via `∃!` (unique existence of factorisation), a substantive Yoneda
100	  bijection content.

137	/-! ## §1. Coherent-sheaf flatness over a base
138	
139	For a morphism `f : X ⟶ S` of schemes and a coherent (more generally:
140	quasi-coherent) `𝓞_X`-module `𝓕`, the *coherent-sheaf flatness* predicate
141	`CoherentSheafFlat f 𝓕` captures Stacks 00HB lifted to the sheaf level:
142	for every point `x ∈ X` the stalk `𝓕_x` is a flat `𝓞_{S, f(x)}`-module
143	via the composite `𝓞_{S, f(x)} → 𝓞_{X, x} → End(𝓕_x)`.
144	
145	Affine-locally (Stacks 00HB equivalence): on an affine open `U = Spec A
146	⊆ S` and an affine open `V = Spec B ⊆ f⁻¹U` with `𝓕|_V = M̃` for a
147	finite `B`-module `M`, the condition becomes `Module.Flat A M` with the
148	`A`-module structure inherited via the ring map `A → B`. The
149	file-internal encoding uses this affine-local form.
150	
151	Blueprint reference: `def:coherent_sheaf_flat`. -/
152	
153	/-- A coherent sheaf `𝓕` on `X` is **flat over `S`** for a morphism
154	`f : X ⟶ S` if, for every affine open `U ⊆ S` and every affine open
155	`V ⊆ f⁻¹U`, the `𝓞_X`-module of sections `Γ(𝓕, V)` is flat as a
156	`Γ(S, U)`-module, where the `Γ(S, U)`-action is the restriction of
157	scalars along the ring morphism `Γ(S, U) → Γ(X, V)` (the `appLE`
158	morphism of `f`).
159	
160	This is the affine-local form of Stacks 00HB (a coherent sheaf is flat
161	over the base if every stalk is flat over the corresponding base
162	stalk); the affine-local form and stalk form are equivalent for
163	*quasi-coherent* sheaves on a noetherian base, which is the regime of
164	the flattening-stratification theorem.
165	
166	iter-177+: the body of subsequent flat-locus theorems may package this
167	predicate as an instance-driven typeclass `Module.Flat`-style if the
168	sheafified-tensor infrastructure lands in Mathlib; for the iter-176
169	file-skeleton it is encoded as a `Prop` quantifier. -/
170	def CoherentSheafFlat {X S : Scheme.{u}} (f : X ⟶ S) (F : X.Modules) : Prop :=
171	  ∀ {U : S.Opens} (_ : IsAffineOpen U) {V : X.Opens} (_ : IsAffineOpen V)
172	    (e : V ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ U),
173	    letI : Module Γ(S, U) Γ(F, V) := Module.compHom _ (f.appLE U V e).hom
174	    Module.Flat Γ(S, U) Γ(F, V)
175	
176	end Scheme

181	`X ⟶ S` is flat above some non-empty open `V ⊆ S`. This is the inductive

20	\section{Setup and motivation}
21	\label{sec:flatstrat_setup}
22	
23	The construction of the Quot scheme \(\Quot_{\F/X/S}^{\Phi,L}\) in Nitsure's exposition
24	of Grothendieck's existence theorem (\cite{nitsure-hilbert-quot}, \S5) proceeds by
25	embedding the functor of points of \(\Quot\) into a Grassmannian via Castelnuovo--Mumford
26	regularity, then cutting out the locus of \emph{quotients with flat fibres of the
27	prescribed Hilbert polynomial} by a closed-subscheme condition.  Cutting out this
28	locus uses, as a primary technical input, the existence of the
29	\emph{flattening stratification} of a coherent sheaf \(\F\) on a projective
30	\(S\)-scheme: a finite collection of locally-closed subschemes of \(S\) on each of which
31	the restriction of \(\F\) becomes flat.
32	
33	This chapter packages the Nitsure~\S4 flattening-stratification theorem as the
34	foundational A.2.a building block. The Quot-scheme construction itself (A.2.b),
35	the relative Picard functor (A.1.c), and the assembly of the Picard scheme
36	representability theorem on a smooth proper curve (A.2.c) live in separate sibling
37	chapters.
38	
39	We work throughout with a noetherian base scheme \(S\), a projective morphism
40	\(\pi : X \to S\), and a coherent \(\OO_X\)-module \(\F\). The conclusion of Nitsure's
41	theorem covers an arbitrary noetherian base with a projective \(X \to S\), which
42	specialises directly to the Route~A consumer setting where \(S = T\) is an arbitrary
43	\(k\)-scheme, \(X = C \times_k T\) for \(C\) a smooth proper curve over the base field
44	\(k\), and \(\F\) is a coherent quotient sheaf produced by the Grassmannian embedding
45	of the Quot functor (cf.\ \ below).
46	
47	\section{Coherent flatness over a base}
48	\label{sec:coh_flat}
49	
50	\begin{definition}
51	
52	\leanok
53	[Coherent sheaf flat over the base]
54	  \label{def:coherent_sheaf_flat}
55	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat}
56	  Let \(S\) be a noetherian scheme, \(f : X \to S\) a morphism of finite type, and
57	  \(\F\) a coherent \(\OO_X\)-module.  We say \(\F\) is \emph{flat over \(S\)}
58	  (equivalently, \(\F\) is \emph{\(f\)-flat}, or \emph{\(S\)-flat}) if for every
59	  point \(x \in X\) the stalk \(\F_x\) is a flat \(\OO_{S, f(x)}\)-module, where the
60	  \(\OO_{S, f(x)}\)-module structure is given by the composite
61	  \(\OO_{S, f(x)} \to \OO_{X, x} \to \End(\F_x)\).
62	  \textit{Source: cf.\ [Stacks Project], tag 00HB (flat modules); [Nitsure], \S4
63	  passim.}
64	  Equivalently (after pulling back to an affine open \(U = \Spec A \subseteq S\) and
65	  an affine open \(V = \Spec B \subseteq f^{-1}(U)\) with \(\F|_V = \widetilde{M}\)
66	  for \(M\) a finite \(B\)-module), \(\F|_V\) is \(\OO_U\)-flat in the sense that \(M\) is a
67	  flat \(A\)-module (with the \(A\)-module structure coming from the ring map
68	  \(A \to B\)).
69	\end{definition}
70	
71	\begin{remark}
72	\label{rem:coh_flat_pullback}
73	  The condition is stable under base change: for any morphism \(g : S' \to S\),
74	  pulling back along the cartesian square
75	  \[
76	    \begin{tikzcd}
77	      X' \ar[r, "g'"] \ar[d, "f'"'] & X \ar[d, "f"] \\
78	      S' \ar[r, "g"]                & S
79	    \end{tikzcd}
80	  \]
81	  yields a coherent \(\OO_{X'}\)-module \(\F' := (g')^* \F\) which is \(S'\)-flat
82	  whenever \(\F\) is \(S\)-flat.  This is the routine cancellation of tensor-product
83	  associativity at the level of stalks. The converse fails in general.
84	\end{remark}
85	
86	\section{Generic flatness}
87	\label{sec:generic_flatness}
88	
89	The construction of the flattening stratification proceeds by Noetherian
90	induction on \(S\). The induction step relies on the following classical
91	\emph{generic flatness} theorem of Grothendieck: over a noetherian integral
92	base, a coherent sheaf is flat over a non-empty open subscheme. Algebraically,
93	this is the assertion that a finitely generated module over a finite-type
94	algebra over a noetherian domain is generically free after inverting a single
95	non-zero element of the base ring.
96	
97	\begin{theorem}
98	[Generic flatness, algebraic form]
99	  \label{thm:generic_flatness_algebraic}
100	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.TODO.genericFlatnessAlgebraic}
101	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S4 ``Lemma on Generic Flatness''
102	  % (read from references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L1711-L1716).
103	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
104	  % "Let \(A\) be a noetherian domain,
105	  %  and \(B\) a finite type \(A\) algebra. Let \(M\) be a finite \(B\)-module.
106	  %  Then there exists an \(f\in A\), \(f\ne 0\), such that
107	  %  the localisation \(M_f\) is a free module over \(A_f\)."
108	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S4, ``Lemma on Generic Flatness''.}
109	  Let \(A\) be a noetherian domain, \(B\) a finite-type \(A\)-algebra, and \(M\) a finite
110	  \(B\)-module. Then there exists \(f \in A\), \(f \ne 0\), such that the localisation
111	  \(M_f\) is a free \(A_f\)-module.
112	\end{theorem}
113	
114	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "Over the quotient field \(K\) of \(A\), the \(K\)-algebra
115	% \(B_K = K\otimes _AB\) is of finite type, and \(M_K = K\otimes_AM\) is a
116	% finite module over \(B_K\). Let \(n\) be the dimension of the support
117	% of \(M_K\) over \(\spec B_K\). We argue by induction on \(n\), starting
118	% with \(n=-1\) which is the case when \(M_K =0\). [...]
119	% Now let \(n\ge 0\), and let the lemma be proved for smaller values.
120	% As \(B\) is noetherian and \(M\) is assumed to be a finite \(B\)-module,
121	% there exists a finite filtration
122	% \(0=M_0 \subset \ldots  \subset M_r =M\)
123	% where each \(M_i\) is a \(B\)-submodule of \(M\) such that for each \(i\ge 1\)
124	% the quotient module \(M_i/M_{i-1}\) is isomorphic to \(B/\pp_i\) for
125	% some prime ideal \(\pp_i\) in \(B\). [...] By Noether normalisation lemma,
126	% there exist elements \(b_1,\ldots,b_n \in B\), such that \(K\otimes_AB\) is
127	% finite over its subalgebra \(K[b_1,\ldots, b_n]\) [...]"
128	% (Source: nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L1719-L1772; full
129	% proof omitted for length, structural steps restated below in project notation.)
130	\begin{proof}
131	  
132	  Following [Nitsure], \S4: pass to the fraction field \(K = \mathrm{Frac}(A)\) and
133	  set \(B_K = K \otimes_A B\), \(M_K = K \otimes_A M\), \(n := \dim \mathrm{supp}_{\Spec B_K} M_K\).
134	  Argue by induction on \(n\), with base case \(n = -1\) (i.e.\ \(M_K = 0\)): each
135	  generator of \(M\) is annihilated by some non-zero element of \(A\); taking the
136	  product of finitely many annihilators yields \(f \in A\) with \(f M = 0\), hence
137	  \(M_f = 0\) is trivially free.  For \(n \ge 0\), a prime filtration of \(M\) as a
138	  finite \(B\)-module
139	  \[
140	    0 = M_0 \subset M_1 \subset \cdots \subset M_r = M
141	  \]
142	  with successive quotients \(M_i / M_{i-1} \cong B / \mathfrak{p}_i\) reduces the
143	  problem (via the splicing fact: \(M_{f'f''}\) is \(A_{f'f''}\)-free when \(M'_{f'}\)
144	  and \(M''_{f''}\) are free) to the case \(M = B/\mathfrak{p}\) for a prime
145	  \(\mathfrak{p} \subset B\), then to the case \(B\) a domain and \(M = B\).
146	  Noether normalisation applied to \(B_K / K\) yields algebraically independent
147	  \(b_1, \dots, b_n \in B\) with \(B_K\) finite over \(K[b_1, \dots, b_n]\); choosing
148	  \(g \ne 0\) to clear denominators of integrality equations, \(B_g\) is finite over
149	  \(A_g[b_1, \dots, b_n]\). Let \(m\) be the generic rank: the short exact sequence
150	  \[
151	    0 \to A_g[b_1, \dots, b_n]^{\oplus m} \to B_g \to T \to 0
152	  \]
153	  has \(T\) a finite torsion module over \(A_g[b_1, \dots, b_n]\), so $\dim
154	  \mathrm{supp}_{B_K} (K \otimes_{A_g} T) < n$.  The inductive hypothesis
155	  yields \(h \ne 0\) in \(A\) with \(T_h\) free over \(A_{gh}\); set \(f := gh\).
156	\end{proof}

159

161	[Generic flatness, geometric form]
162	  \label{thm:generic_flatness}
163	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.genericFlatness}
164	  \uses{thm:generic_flatness_algebraic}
165	
166	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S4, Theorem ``generic flatness''
167	  % (read from references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L1781-L1787).
168	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
169	  % "Let \(S\) be a noetherian and integral scheme.
170	  %  Let \(p: X\to S\) be a finite type morphism, and let \(\F\) be a
171	  %  coherent sheaf of \({\mathcal O}_X\)-modules. Then there exists a non-empty
172	  %  open subscheme \(U\subset S\) such that the restriction of \(\F\)
173	  %  to \(X_U = p^{-1}(U)\) is flat over \({\mathcal O}_U\)."
174	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S4, Theorem ``generic flatness''.}
175	  Let \(S\) be a noetherian integral scheme, \(p : X \to S\) a finite-type morphism,
176	  and \(\F\) a coherent \(\OO_X\)-module. Then there exists a non-empty open
177	  subscheme \(U \subseteq S\) such that the restriction of \(\F\) to
178	  \(X_U = p^{-1}(U)\) is \(\OO_U\)-flat.
179	\end{theorem}
180	
181	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "The above theorem has the following consequence, which
182	% follows by restricting attention to a non-empty affine open subscheme of \(S\)."
183	% (Source: nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L1776-L1777. The
184	% Nitsure proof is the single sentence quoted: deduce the geometric form from
185	% Theorem~REF by restricting to a non-empty
186	% affine open of \(S\), where the situation reduces to the algebraic statement.)
187	\begin{proof}
188	  
189	  Restrict to a non-empty affine open \(\Spec A \subseteq S\) where \(A\) is a
190	  noetherian domain. The morphism \(p\) is of finite type, so locally on \(X\)
191	  above this affine \(\F\) is the sheaf \(\widetilde{M}\) for \(M\) a finite module
192	  over a finite-type \(A\)-algebra \(B\). Theorem~REF
193	  produces \(f \in A\), \(f \ne 0\), with \(M_f\) free over \(A_f\) on each such patch;
194	  shrinking to the basic open \(D(f) \subseteq \Spec A\) globalises by patching
195	  finitely many such opens (using noetherianity of \(X\) above the affine).
196	\end{proof}

246	  Let \(S\) be a noetherian scheme and \(\F\) a coherent sheaf on the relative
247	  projective space \(\PP^n_S\) over \(S\).  Then:
248	  \begin{enumerate}[label=(\roman*)]
249	    \item The set \(I\) of Hilbert polynomials
250	      \(P_s(m) = \chi(\PP^n_s, \F_s(m))\) that arise as \(s\) ranges over \(S\) is
251	      finite.
252	    \item For each \(f \in I\) there exists a locally-closed subscheme
253	      \(S_f \hookrightarrow S\) whose underlying set \(|S_f|\) consists of exactly
254	      those \(s \in S\) with \(P_s = f\).  The collection \(\{|S_f|\}_{f \in I}\) is
255	      pairwise disjoint with set-theoretic union \(|S|\).  Forming the coproduct
256	      \(S' := \coprod_{f \in I} S_f\) and the morphism \(i : S' \to S\) assembled
257	      from the inclusions \(S_f \hookrightarrow S\), the pullback \(i^* \F\) on
258	      \(\PP^n_{S'}\) is flat over \(S'\), and \(i : S' \to S\) is universal with this
259	      property: for any morphism \(\varphi : T \to S\) such that \(\varphi^* \F\) on
260	      \(\PP^n_T\) is flat over \(T\), \(\varphi\) factors uniquely through \(i\).
261	      Each \(S_f\) is uniquely determined by \(f\).
262	    \item Order \(I\) by \(f < g \iff f(n) < g(n)\) for all \(n \gg 0\). Then the
263	      closure of \(|S_f|\) in \(|S|\) is contained in \(\bigcup_{g \ge f} |S_g|\).
264	  \end{enumerate}
265	\end{theorem}

279	\begin{proof}
280	  \uses{def:coherent_sheaf_flat, lem:flat_locus_open, lem:nonflat_locus_proper,
281	    lem:noetherian_induction_strata, thm:generic_flatness}
282	  Following [Nitsure], \S4, the construction reduces to the special case
283	  \(n = 0\), assembled over the relative twists \(\pi_* \F(N+i)\).  As the
284	  argument is local on \(S\) (the strata glue by their universal property, part
285	  (ii)), and as the three conclusions are proved through the sub-lemmas below,
286	  we assemble them in the order (i), (ii), (iii).
287	
288	  \emph{Part (i): finiteness of the Hilbert-polynomial set.}
289	  By Lemma~\ref{lem:nonflat_locus_proper}, Noetherian induction on \(S\) (whose
290	  inductive step invokes generic flatness, Theorem~\ref{thm:generic_flatness},
291	  on the integral locally-closed strata cut out by the irreducible-component
292	  decomposition of \(S\)) produces finitely many reduced, pairwise disjoint,
293	  locally-closed subschemes \(V_i \hookrightarrow S\) set-theoretically covering
294	  \(|S|\) on each of which \(\F|_{\PP^n_{V_i}}\) is flat over \(\OO_{V_i}\) in
295	  the sense of Definition~\ref{def:coherent_sheaf_flat}.  On a flat family the
296	  Hilbert polynomial \(P_s(m) = \chi(\PP^n_s, \F_s(m))\) is locally constant, so
297	  it takes finitely many values on each quasi-compact \(V_i\); the finite union
298	  over the finitely many \(V_i\) shows that the set \(I\) of Hilbert polynomials
299	  occurring on \(S\) is finite.
300	
301	  \emph{Part (ii): existence of the strata and their universal property.}
302	  Working over the flat pieces \(V_i\) of part (i), semicontinuity
303	  (cohomology-and-base-change) yields a uniform integer \(N\) such that, for all
304	  \(s \in S\) and \(m \ge N\), the higher cohomologies \(H^r(\PP^n_s, \F_s(m))\)
305	  vanish for \(r \ge 1\) and the base-change homomorphism
306	  \((\pi_* \F(m))|_s \to H^0(\PP^n_s, \F_s(m))\) is an isomorphism.  With this
307	  \(N\) fixed, Lemma~\ref{lem:noetherian_induction_strata} applies the \(n = 0\)
308	  stratification mechanism (Lemma~\ref{lem:flat_locus_open}) successively to the
309	  coherent sheaves \(E_i = \pi_* \F(N+i)\) on \(S\) for \(i = 0, \dots, n\),
310	  refining stratum by stratum.  After \(n+1\) steps this produces a finite
311	  family of locally-closed subschemes \(W_{e_0, \dots, e_n} \subseteq S\),
312	  universal for the property that each \(E_i\) pulls back to a locally-free sheaf
313	  of constant rank \(e_i\); re-indexing the tuple \((e_0, \dots, e_n)\) by the
314	  unique numerical polynomial \(f\) of degree \(\le n\) with \(f(N+i) = e_i\)
315	  gives the family \(\{W_f\}_{f \in I}\).  At any \(s \in W_f\) the base-change
316	  isomorphism and the cohomology vanishing force \(P_s = f\) (since \(P_s\) has
317	  degree \(\le n\) and is determined by its values at \(N, \dots, N+n\)), so
318	  \(|W_f| = \{s : P_s = f\}\); these subsets are disjoint with union \(|S|\).
319	  The required locally-closed subscheme \(S_f \subseteq W_f\) is the closed
320	  subscheme cut out by the further local-freeness conditions
321	  ``\(\pi_* \F(N+i)|_{W_f}\) is locally free of rank \(f(N+i)\) for all
322	  \(i \ge 0\)'' (a chain of coherent ideal sheaves that stabilises by
323	  Noetherianity), and \(\F|_{\PP^n_{S_f}}\) is flat over \(S_f\)
324	  (Definition~\ref{def:coherent_sheaf_flat}) because the graded module
325	  \(\bigoplus_{i \gg 0} \pi_* \F(i)|_{S_f}\) is locally free.  Forming
326	  \(S' = \coprod_{f \in I} S_f\) and the assembled morphism \(i : S' \to S\),
327	  the pullback \(i^* \F\) is \(S'\)-flat; and any \(\varphi : T \to S\) with
328	  \(\varphi^* \F\) flat over \(T\) has locally constant Hilbert polynomial, hence
329	  decomposes \(T = \coprod_f T_f\) into the open-closed loci where the polynomial
330	  is \(f\), each factoring uniquely through the corresponding \(S_f\) by the
331	  local-freeness universal property of Lemma~\ref{lem:flat_locus_open}.  This
332	  gives the unique factorisation through \(i\), and shows each \(S_f\) is
333	  uniquely determined by \(f\).
334	
335	  \emph{Part (iii): closure of strata.}
336	  Order \(I\) by \(f < g \iff f(n) < g(n)\) for all \(n \gg 0\).  Since each
337	  \(S_f\) is a stratum of the \(n = 0\) flattening stratification applied to a
338	  single direct image \(\pi_* \F(p)\) for \(p \gg 0\) separating all polynomials
339	  in \(I\), the closure-of-strata assertion reduces to the upper-semicontinuity
340	  statement already established in the \(n = 0\) case
341	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:flat_locus_open}): the closure of \(|S_f|\) in \(|S|\) is
342	  contained in \(\bigcup_{g \ge f} |S_g|\).
343	\end{proof}

351	isomorphisms for the direct images \(\pi_* \F(m)\), which allow comparing

394	  Let \(S\) be a noetherian scheme and let \(\F\) be a coherent \(\OO_S\)-module.
395	  For each integer \(e \ge 0\), there exists a locally-closed subscheme
396	  \(S_e \hookrightarrow S\) whose underlying set is
397	  \(\{s \in S : \dim_{\kappa(s)} \F|_s = e\}\). The family \(\{S_e\}_e\) is a
398	  finite, mutually disjoint, set-theoretically covering stratification of \(S\),
399	  and a morphism \(f : T \to S\) pulls back \(\F\) to a locally-free
400	  \(\OO_T\)-module of rank \(e\) if and only if \(f\) factors through \(S_e\).  The
401	  function \(s \mapsto \dim_{\kappa(s)} \F|_s\) is upper-semicontinuous on \(S\).

404	\begin{proof}
405	  
406	  By [Nitsure], \S4 (verbatim above): for \(s \in S\), Nakayama produces a
407	  neighbourhood \(V\) of \(s\) and a right-exact local presentation
408	  \(\OO_V^{\oplus m} \xrightarrow{\psi} \OO_V^{\oplus e} \to \F|_V \to 0\) where
409	  \(e = \dim_{\kappa(s)} \F|_s\).  The closed subscheme \(V_e \hookrightarrow V\)
410	  defined by the ideal sheaf generated by the matrix entries of \(\psi\)
411	  represents the locus where \(\F\) becomes locally free of rank \(e\): by
412	  right-exactness of tensor product, a base change \(f : T \to V\) has
413	  \(f^* \F\) locally free of rank \(e\) iff \(f^* \psi = 0\), iff \(f\) factors
414	  through \(V_e\).  Glueing the \(V_e\) over the cover of \(S\) by such
415	  neighbourhoods (and intersecting with \(V \setminus \bigcup_{e' > e} V_{e'}\)
416	  to get the strictly-equal-to-\(e\) stratum) produces the locally-closed
417	  \(S_e\). Upper-semicontinuity of \(e\) follows because the rank of \(\psi\) is
418	  lower-semicontinuous.
419	\end{proof}

555	\begin{proof}
556	  
557	  By Lemma~REF, the family of Hilbert polynomials
558	  \(\{P_s\}\) on \(S\) is finite, and there is a finite stratification
559	  \(\{V_i\}\) of \(S\) on each of which \(\F\) is flat with locally-constant
560	  Hilbert polynomial.  Apply semi-continuity (cohomology and base change,
561	  [Hartshorne], III.12 / [Stacks Project], tag 02KH for the \(H^0\)-version) on
562	  the noetherian \(V_i\) to obtain a uniform integer \(N_1\) with
563	  \(R^r \pi_* \F(m) = 0\) for \(r \ge 1\), \(m \ge N_1\), and \(H^r(\PP^n_s, \F_s(m)) = 0\)
564	  for all \(s \in S\), \(r \ge 1\), \(m \ge N_1\) (statement (B) of Nitsure's proof).
565	  Choose \(r_i \ge N_1\) on each \(V_i\) such that the base-change comparison
566	  \((\pi_* \F(m))|_{V_i} \to (\pi_i)_* \F_{V_i}(m)\) is an isomorphism for
567	  \(m \ge r_i\); composing with the cohomology-and-base-change isomorphism
568	  \(((\pi_i)_* \F_{V_i}(m))|_s \xrightarrow{\sim} H^0(\PP^n_s, \F_s(m))\) for
569	  the flat family on \(V_i\) yields uniform \(N = \max r_i\) such that
570	  \((\pi_* \F(m))|_s \to H^0(\PP^n_s, \F_s(m))\) is an isomorphism for \(m \ge N\)
571	  and all \(s \in S\) (statement (C)).
572	
573	  Apply Lemma~REF sequentially to the coherent sheaves
574	  \(E_i = \pi_* \F(N+i)\) on \(S\) for \(i = 0, \dots, n\): \(E_0\) produces a
575	  stratification \(\{W_{e_0}\}\) of \(S\); on each \(W_{e_0}\) apply the lemma to
576	  \(E_1|_{W_{e_0}}\) to refine to \(\{W_{e_0, e_1}\}\), and continue. After \(n+1\)
577	  steps the \(W_{e_0, \dots, e_n}\) form a finite locally-closed stratification
578	  of \(S\) universal for the property ``each \(E_i\) pulls back to a locally-free
579	  \(\OO_T\)-module of constant rank \(e_i\)''.  Re-index \((e_0, \dots, e_n)\) by the
580	  unique numerical polynomial \(f\) of degree \(\le n\) with \(f(N+i) = e_i\) to get
581	  the family \(\{W_f\}\) indexed by polynomials.  At any \(s \in W_f\), statement
582	  (C) and vanishing (B) force \(P_s = f\) (since \(P_s\) has degree \(\le n\) and is
583	  determined by its values at \(N, N+1, \dots, N+n\)).
584	
585	  The Hilbert-polynomial-coincidence locus \(|W_f|\) may have non-flat scheme
586	  structure; refine to \(S_f \subseteq W_f\) by intersecting with the
587	  closed-subscheme conditions ``\(\pi_* \F(N+i)|_{W_f}\) is locally-free of rank
588	  \(f(N+i)\)'' for all \(i \ge 0\).  These conditions are cut out by an increasing
589	  chain of coherent ideal sheaves \(I_0 \subseteq I_0 + I_1 \subseteq \cdots\) on
590	  \(W_f\); Noetherianity terminates the chain in finitely many steps, defining a
591	  coherent ideal sheaf \(I\) and a closed subscheme \(S_f \subseteq W_f\) with
592	  \(|S_f| = |W_f|\) on which \(\pi_* \F(N+i)\) is locally-free of rank \(f(N+i)\)
593	  for all \(i \ge 0\). The base-change-without-flatness lemma (cf.\ [Nitsure],
594	  \S3) yields some \(N' \ge N\) such that for \(i \ge N'\),
595	  \((\pi_* \F(i))|_{S_f} \to (\pi_{S_f})_* \F_{S_f}(i)\) is an isomorphism, hence
596	  the graded \(A\)-module \(\bigoplus_{i \ge N'} (\pi_{S_f})_* \F_{S_f}(i)\) is
597	  locally-free on \(S_f\), hence \(\F_{S_f}\) is flat over \(S_f\). The
598	  closure-of-strata property (iii) reduces to the corresponding property in
599	  the \(n=0\) case for \(\pi_* \F(p)\) on \(S\) at any \(p \ge N\) separating all
600	  Hilbert polynomials.
601	\end{proof}
602	
603	\section{Universal property of the strata}
604	\label{sec:flatstrat_universal}
605	
606	\begin{theorem}
607	
608	\leanok
609	[Universal property of the flattening stratification]
610	  \label{thm:flattening_stratification_universal}
611	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.flatteningStratification_universal}
612	  \uses{thm:flattening_stratification_exists, lem:noetherian_induction_strata}
613	
614	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S4, Theorem ``flattening stratification'', part (ii)
615	  % universal property
616	  % (read from references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L1826-L1834).
617	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
618	  % "\textbf{(ii) Universal property: }
619	  %  Let \(S'=\coprod S_f\) be the coproduct of the \(S_f\), and let
620	  %  \(i:S'\to S\) be the morphism induced by the inclusions
621	  %  \(S_f\hra S\). Then the sheaf \(i^*(\F)\) on \(\PP^n_{S'}\)
622	  %  is flat over \(S'\). Moreover, \(i:S'\to S\) has the universal property that
623	  %  for any morphism \(\varphi :T\to S\) the pullback \(\varphi^*(\F)\) on
624	  %  \(\PP^n_T\) is flat over \(T\) if and only if \(\varphi\) factors through
625	  %  \(i:S'\to S\).  The subscheme \(S_f\) is
626	  %  uniquely determined by the polynomial \(f\)."
627	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S4 (part (ii) of the main theorem); cf.\ [Stacks
628	  Project], tag 052H.}
629	  Let \(S\), \(\F\), and \(\{S_f\}_{f \in I}\) be as in
630	  Theorem~REF.  Set
631	  \(S' := \coprod_{f \in I} S_f\) with the coproduct morphism
632	  \(i : S' \to S\) assembled from the locally-closed inclusions.  Then \(i^* \F\)
633	  is flat over \(S'\), and the pair \((S', i)\) is universal: for any morphism
634	  \(\varphi : T \to S\) of noetherian schemes (one may take \(T\) arbitrary, the
635	  Noetherian hypothesis on \(S\) being preserved), the pullback
636	  \(\varphi^* \F\) on \(\PP^n_T\) is \(T\)-flat if and only if \(\varphi\) factors
637	  uniquely through \(i\). In particular each \(S_f\) is uniquely determined by \(f\).
638	\end{theorem}
639	
640	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "We now show that the morphism \(\coprod_f S_f \,\to \,S\)
641	% indeed has the property (ii) of the theorem.
642	% By Lemma REF,
643	% there exists some \(N' \ge N\) such that for all \(i\ge N'\),
644	% the base-change
645	% \((\pi_*\F(i))|_{S_f} \to (\pi_{S_f})_*\F_{S_f}(i)\) is an isomorphism for
646	% each \(S_f\). Therefore
647	% \(\F_{S_f}\) is flat over \(S_f\) by Lemma REF,
648	% as the direct images \(\pi_*\F(i)\) for all \(i \ge N'\) are locally
649	% free over \(S_f\). Conversely, if \(\phi : T\to S\) is a morphism such
650	% that \(\F_T\) is flat, then the Hilbert polynomial is locally
651	% constant over \(T\). Let \(T_f\) be the open and closed subscheme of \(T\)
652	% where the Hilbert polynomial is \(f\). Clearly, the set
653	% map \(|T_f| \to |S|\) factors via \(|S_f|\). But as the direct images
654	% \({\pi_T}_*\F_T(i)\) are locally free of rank \(f(i)\) on \(T_f\), it
655	% follows in fact that the schematic morphism \(T_f \to S\) factors
656	% via \(S_f\), proving the property (ii) of the theorem."
657	% (Source: nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L2065-L2082.)
658	\begin{proof}
659	  
660	  Flatness of \(i^* \F\) over \(S'\) was established at the end of the proof of
661	  Theorem~REF (the
662	  base-change-without-flatness lemma plus the graded-module-flatness criterion
663	  applied to the coherent sheaves \(\pi_* \F(N+i)\) on each \(S_f\)).
664	  Conversely, if \(\varphi : T \to S\) satisfies \(\varphi^* \F\) on \(\PP^n_T\) is
665	  \(T\)-flat, then \(T\)-flatness of the family forces the Hilbert polynomial to be
666	  locally constant on \(T\).  Decompose \(T = \coprod_f T_f\) into the open-closed
667	  subschemes where the Hilbert polynomial is \(f\).  The set map \(|T_f| \to |S|\)
668	  factors through \(|S_f|\); further, since the direct images
669	  \((\pi_T)_* \varphi^* \F(i)\) are locally-free of rank \(f(i)\) on \(T_f\) for $i
670	  \ge N'\(, the universal property of \)S_f \subseteq W_f$ as the closed
671	  subscheme cut out by these local-freeness conditions
672	  (Lemma~REF) yields a unique scheme
673	  morphism \(T_f \to S_f\) extending the set map. Assembling over \(f\) gives the
674	  unique factorisation \(\varphi = i \circ \tilde \varphi\).
675	\end{proof}
676	
677	\section{Application: relative curves}
678	\label{sec:flatstrat_curve_application}
679	
680	In the Route~A consumer setting, the input to the flattening-stratification
681	machinery is the pullback \(C \times_k T \to T\) where \(C\) is a smooth proper
682	curve over a field \(k\) and \(T\) is any \(k\)-scheme.  The hypotheses of
683	Nitsure's Theorem~REF demand
684	\emph{projective} relative dimension, not merely \emph{proper}; we record that
685	no extra work is required to instantiate the theorem in our setting.
686	
687	\begin{lemma}
688	[Smooth proper curve over \(k\) is projective]
689	  \label{lem:smooth_proper_curve_projective}
690	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.TODO.smoothProperCurveProjective}
691	  % SOURCE: [Hartshorne], II.6.7 / II.7.6 (curve \(\Rightarrow\) quasi-projective;
692	  % proper \(\Rightarrow\) projective for curves), and standard consequences in
693	  % chapter III. The precise statement ``every smooth proper curve over a field
694	  % is projective'' is one of the classical equivalences for \(\dim 1\) and is
695	  % textbook material; this block is project-bespoke (no verbatim source
696	  % quote is reproduced here as the result is folklore-level and cited only
697	  % structurally for the Route~A reduction).
698	  \textit{Source: classical; cf.\ [Hartshorne], II.6.7 \& II.7.6 (curves).}
699	  Let \(C\) be a smooth proper curve over a field \(k\).  Then \(C \to \Spec k\) is
700	  projective.  Consequently, for any noetherian \(k\)-scheme \(T\), the relative
701	  curve \(C \times_k T \to T\) is projective in the sense of Grothendieck (there
702	  exists a relatively very ample line bundle on \(C \times_k T\) over \(T\)).
703	\end{lemma}
704	
705	\begin{proof}
706	  Projectivity of a smooth proper curve over a field is classical
707	  ([Hartshorne], II.6.7 / II.7.6; alternatively: every divisor of large
708	  positive degree on a smooth proper curve \(C\) is very ample, providing the
709	  embedding \(C \hookrightarrow \PP^N_k\) for some \(N\)). Pulling the ample line
710	  bundle back along \(C \times_k T \to C\) produces a relatively very ample line
711	  bundle on \(C \times_k T\) over \(T\).
712	\end{proof}
713	
714	\begin{corollary}
715	
716	\leanok
717	[Flattening stratification for a coherent sheaf on a relative curve]
718	  \label{cor:flattening_stratification_curve}
719	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.flatteningStratification.ofCurve}
720	  \uses{lem:smooth_proper_curve_projective, thm:flattening_stratification_exists}
721	  Let \(k\) be a field, \(C\) a smooth proper curve over \(k\), and \(T\) a noetherian
722	  \(k\)-scheme.  Let \(\F\) be a coherent sheaf on \(C \times_k T\). Then the
723	  flattening-stratification conclusion of
724	  Theorem~REF applies: there is a finite
725	  set \(I\) of Hilbert polynomials and locally-closed subschemes
726	  \(T_f \hookrightarrow T\) for \(f \in I\), set-theoretically covering \(T\)
727	  disjointly, such that (a) \(\F|_{C \times_k T_f}\) is flat over \(T_f\) for
728	  each \(f\), and (b) any morphism \(\varphi : T' \to T\) such that
729	  \(\varphi^* \F\) on \(C \times_k T'\) is \(T'\)-flat factors uniquely through some
730	  \(T_f\).
731	\end{corollary}
732	
733	\begin{proof}
734	  
735	  By Lemma~REF, \(C \times_k T \to T\) is
736	  projective, hence factors through a closed immersion into \(\PP^n_T\) for some
737	  \(n \ge 0\).  Push \(\F\) forward along the closed immersion (extending by zero
738	  outside the image) to a coherent sheaf \(\tilde \F\) on \(\PP^n_T\); this
739	  operation preserves \(T\)-flatness in both directions because the closed
740	  immersion is a finite morphism.  Apply
741	  Theorem~REF to \(\tilde \F\) on
742	  \(\PP^n_T\) to obtain \(\{T_f\}\) with the stated universal property.
743	\end{proof}
